note: Simple Add More is a small, JS-driven field-widget UX module: one config object, one permission, one alter hook, a fixed allow-list of widget types, and a per-widget third-party opt-out. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, what does the 'Simple Add More' (sam) module do to a multi-value field form, and does it apply to unlimited-cardinality fields?
evals.expected_output: It hides surplus empty elements on fixed limited-cardinality (> 1) fields, showing one empty element plus an 'Add another item' button. It does NOT apply to unlimited (-1) or single-value fields.

evals.prompt: Which config object and key control the label of the 'Add another item' button in the sam (Simple Add More) module, and what is its default value?
evals.expected_output: The add_more_label key on the sam.settings config object; default 'Add another item'.

evals.prompt: What is the route name and admin path of the Simple Add More settings form on a Drupal site?
Which permission does the sam (Simple Add More) module define to gate its settings form?
evals.expected_output: The 'administer sam' permission gates the Simple Add More settings form.

evals.prompt: I have a custom field widget and want the Simple Add More module to simplify it. Which hook do I implement to add my widget to the supported list?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_sam_allowed_widget_types_alter(array &$widget_types) and append the widget's plugin id.

evals.prompt: How do I disable Simple Add More for just one specific widget on one form display without turning off the module? Name the setting.
evals.expected_output: Check the 'Skip Simple Add More simplification' box on the widget in Manage form display; stored as the third-party setting sam.skip_simplification (boolean).

evals.prompt: In the Simple Add More help-text settings (help_text_singular / help_text_plural), what placeholder token inserts the number of remaining addable items?
evals.expected_output: The @count placeholder, e.g. '@count additional items can be added'.
